Main problem. A month ago I was cooking using one burner set at 3. I heard a loud pop and no power to the cooktop. I checked the breaker, wires. No issue found. Once I opened the unit I found a blown fuse and the burnt trace.
I contacted them about it but unfortunately my warranty was expired. 1 year. I used the cooktop for an extra 6 months.
I have to say the gentleman on the email was kind enough to provide a free replacement board. Well,
3 times they sent me the wrong parts. 3 times. I provided photos and part numbers on what i needed. Shipping? It does not work either. They creat a label on Monday but it ships Friday. 3 times. Replacement parts are not available anywhere according to customers service.
Sorry gasland but....never buy this again.

title: Does not work
text:
I’ve had this Ice maker for less than a month and it is already not working properly. It started out working fine making ice pellets like described— however my initial issue was that the ice did not remain cold in the maker, the ice melts moments after being made because there is no insulation. When I received it I couldn’t figure out how to use it because it didn’t make sense to me that you would put water in the basin that appeared to be where the ice would be stored.

It all made sense once the ice started melting and recycling to become ice again. This was only a mild annoyance until the ice started tasting like chemicals, I assume from the melting and refreezing with a the same Freon repeatedly.

I solved this problem by PURCHASING an ice storage been for the freezer and dumping it in there as soon as it was made; and using bottled water rather than tap.

After another week or so, the NEW problem was that the ice was turning into tiny caps, rather than bullets as described. This began yesterday. I thought perhaps the machine needed to defrost, so I unplugged it overnight.

This morning I plugged it back in and the first few rotations were normal ice bullets, then they became shorter, and shorter until they weren’t even ice caps. The images provided are of what they started out looking like and what they looked like before I called Amazon to receive a refund. I didn’t bother to take another picture, but they are even smaller still.

Don’t waste your money.

title: Failed in only 7 months - design defect
text: Only 7 months after installation, this refrigerator defrost heater failed.  Evaluation by an electrical engineer showed that the heating element burned into an open state, apparently due to penetration of water onto the coil surface.  There were multiple failures which caused the coil to burn out:  1) a broken seal; 2) a crack in the protective glass (likely caused by water dripping directly onto the glass tube); and 3) inadequate shielding at the ends of the tube which allowed melting frost to access the glass tube.  We have installed another using aluminum foil to shield the ends of the part.  Whether this will work in the long term remains to be seen.  (2 stars only because it did work for a matter of months; otherwise 1 star due to poor design.)

title: Mine were just stickers, not breathable at all
text:
I used these on jars and as ventilation hole filters but I noticed a lot of condensation build up with these, which led to mold growth. When I went to peel them off I noticed there are three layers: a donut shaped adhesive, round impermeable plastic film, and the filter paper top. Some of the inner plastic films looked like they were punched to be removed but most were solid and blocked the filter layer completely.

I took an empty water bottle and drilled a hole in the cap and put one of these in place. Squeezing the bottle, the sticker would eventually pop off (they DO hold a lot of pressure like the description says, which makes me question if these were supposed to be filters or temporary hole covers). Even water didn’t pass through until the sticker seal started to give out. Maybe I got a defective batch.

title: Connection Tabs Wrong
text:
These pictures show the orginal motor that I replaced in my Frigidaire side-by-side refrigerator. The motor is model 242077702. The second picture shows the wiring connector of the motor. Notice that the inner sides of the locking tabs have rectangular shaped voids. These voids are what allows the connector to securely lock into place when inserting into the other half of the coupling.

This item that was sent was listed as a replacement for this model but the locking tabs were only half the thickness of the original, but more importantly, did not have any "void" or indentation which would snap into place. I had to zip tie the halves of the coupling otherwise they would not stay coupled.

title: Get one with lights in the rear instead.
text:
Installed in May 2022, reviewing in November. Functionally the fan is still working well. Aesthetically it looks awesome. Fan is adequate strength, we always use high-power though... lower power not of much use really.

I chose one with lights in the front thinking it would light the stove up better, but what I didn't think about it is that that also means the fan, which is already less deep than the stove, is set closer to the wall. As a result the fan is much more effective when using the back burners. On the front burner a lot more escapes the fan's pull. The fan being forward another 4-5" would certainly have helped.

Install went fairly well. Overall well designed with 2 flaws that are manageable for someone handy enough to be installing their own vent hood. 1) It comes with a cord but no opening in the chimney shroud to put it through. I cut a hole in the bottom back of the shroud... unfortunately I did this before I realized I had electricity in the attic I could hardwire it with instead (thus the hole in the picture with no cord). 2) The design of the upper shroud segment's mounting does not allow it to mount tight to the ceiling. I decided to hold it tight to the ceiling and then drill and screw it to hold. I used a rigid vent (sold separately).

title: Don't Fit
text:
My own fault, I didn't open these until almost a year after ordering, assuming they'd work. Well, I finally went to replace my old knobs which have loosened over time, and these new knobs are too small (they look like they'd fit but they don't), and the internal piece which dictates which direction they would go on is different than the original knobs. The original knobs point down when they are turned to off. These knobs would look like they're on low (turned a quarter turn to the left). So, not only do they not fit, but the internal piece is facing the wrong way. Overall, a waste of $25 and now back to the frustrating task of trying to find these knobs again.

You can see in the photos the labeling and the way the internal piece inside the stovetop faces. The flat part of the knob needs to be at the top.

Second photo is the current/original knob, and the third photo is the bottom of that knob. You'll notice there are two black dots at the top - this indicates the top of the knob.

The fourth and fifth photos is a new knob - you'll see the black dots are on the left, showing that when the knob is placed correctly, the top of the knob will be pointing left to right instead of up and down.

title: This product did not function properly
text: This fuse could not possibly meet the over current electrical characteristics of the OEM part. This fuse is intended to protect the wiring and circuit board in the dishwasher from shorts in the heating element. My problem occurred during the first washing of a load of dishes. Apparently, my heating element was beginning to fail which is why the original fuse failed. This fuse however, did not trip when the heating element actually failed causing an over current condition. This failure to trip caused overheating in the heating element circuit, specifically, where the wiring connects to the circuit board. This failure rendered the circuit board inoperable (see pics). Now I am faced with having to purchase a $400 circuit board!!!

title: Don't work for all stovetops, but help
text:
We have about a 1/8 inch gap on each side of the stove and liquids/food invariably get spilled in that area. I thought these would fix the problem, and while they help, they don't fix everything.

Installation was easy - slip into the gap and expand to cover the area needed. The flaps can be bent out to hold in place more firmly if needed. You will most likely need to do that as they slide easily.

The main problem for us was two-fold (see the pics):
1) our stove has a raised edge all around to help contain spills and, because of this, the cover doesn't go all the way down to the stovetop in our case. If we have a large spill (like pasta boil-over), the pasta water still got under this cover and into the gap.
2) our stove doesn't have square corners at the back, it curves from the cooking surface up to the control panel. This made it hard to get the cover flush in the back area of the stove about 1 inch from the back. This also led to a spill still getting into the crack.

The stainless was easy to clean and the design is easy to install, but this set would work best on a flat topped stove with square joints coming up for the controls. It helps with spills, but doesn't stop the mess.

title: Properly marked belt was not the actual belt that needed/ordered
text: So the rollers worked just fine and were easy to replace.  It was the belt that was EXTREMELY difficult to mount.  It was shorter than the old belt but thought maybe the old belt had stretched over time.  When I did get the new belt on and reassembled the dryer, I started it and it made a terrible grating/rumbling sound so I immediately turned it off.  After re-disassembling the dryer and removing the new belt, I replaced it with the old belt and re-assembled the dryer and it worked like a charm.  It was then when I took a look at the reviews and saw where others had received a properly marked belt that was actually the wrong belt.  Holding my judgment, I then ordered a whirlpool belt from sears direct.  It arrived today and the pic is a comparison between the two.  The longer red belt is the whirlpool sears belt.  The black one is the belt from this kit.  I will continue to use the original belt until it breaks but I have faith that the whirlpool sears direct belt will work just fine.  If you order this kit, beware of the chance that the parts you ordered might not be the parts you get.

title: Absolutely terrible -  do not buy!
text: I purchased these filters two times hoping that maybe the first time I just got a bad batch.  I  also purchased another size for my other Vornadohumidifier.  After the first week or so of the filter completely started to disintegrate and dries out and turns brown.  I tried flipping it, then the second time I bought them tried not flipping it. Each time even with full water and not ever letting it run out of water, the filter just disintegrates and stop putting moisture in the air. I even bought a humidistat to check to see if it was doing anything and it’s absolutely not. I have the Vornado single canister. It’s not my water because I’ve bought other non-Vornado replacement filters that worked but they were out of stock, so I thought it would be better to have the OEM version.  I was wrong. These are very expensive for one filter at $15 when the entire humidifier only cost $69,  i’ve now spent almost half the cost of the unit and I have to go buy more.  I also have the double tank energy saver Vornado and bought the Vornado  replacement filters for that one, and exact same thing happened. I purchased a non-brand Replacement, and those are working great and have silver mesh to keep them from falling apart, and they don’t dry out or disintegrate.  I’m going to be contacting Votnado directly for a refund since the Amazon window is only 30 days which I missed by a day.  I absolutely would not recommend these filters,  and with all the poor reviews I cannot believe Amazon continues to sell these, or Vornado has not done something to make this right.  The first two photos are the single canister, and the second are the devil canister with the non-Varnado shelters that work great. Those are also from Amazon

title: Maytag has Cheeped Out on their word on strong quality Appliances.
text: I have always been a Maytag customer for their strong and high quality of their appliances. But a few years ago they were bought out.  Now we will find different parts from Whirl Pool in their equipment.  this is one of them. As it has already stated by another review about the life of the part, it will go about 1 year before some of the options start to go out.  This is our 2nd control panel we have bought.  the original only lasted 2 years before all of the options stopped working.  As for the 2nd panel  it lasted about 6 years, but the last 4 years only the normal and start options worked. Which was good enough for us.  The reasons why they do not last is because WP did not design the flat wire harness to be able to carry the currant load to and from the control panel.  it is made up of 2 different type harnesses. one standard hard line wire and then the flat harness which is used in this type of control panel. they tried to put Maytag old school of Quality with future technology in touch control versus hard line contact switches. As you can see in the picture the flat wire overheated, broke through the air tight coating and oxidized and then flaked away. There are ways around this, they could have replaced this panel with an old school quality type rather than just leaving it up to the customer to deal with it.  One has to be very carful when mixing old school with new school.  nothing wrong with ether of them as long as they are design to perform to the customers satisfaction and needs. In Short, Maytag is no longer Maytag any more.  Sad, very sad.

text:

It fits into the socket, but the opening where the water flows through is noticeably smaller than the official GE filter's (Photo: EcoAqua on the left, GE on the right). Because of this, water flow through the filter is either severely reduced or stopped altogether. The fridge's pump is very loud when it tries forcing water through, which I'm sure isn't good for it. On top of that the water tastes like it came straight out of my garden hose, even after disposing of the first 2 gallons like the filter recommends; which is why I suspect water isn't even passing through the filter.

Some refrigerators may have different specs that this would be more compatible with, but this was a total fail for mine.

title: Disappointing in a Vertuo Next, kept floating up after a couple of hours
text:
I was dubious about a generic filter for a Nespresso machine. The filter assembly for my Keurig is built for the water tank design, so this didn't seem as likely to work seamlessly. Spoiler: It didn't.

The first thing I noticed was that the charcoal pods were different sizes. Noticeably, like at least 1/8 of an inch. I didn't see any replacement filters for it, so I was optimistically expecting the kit to last three months.

The first morning I woke up to the machine after installing the filter, it was floating at the top of the water tank. I didn't get a photo of it, as it was before my first cuppa, but I pushed it back down, thought it was well stuck, but 2 hours later it had floated again. After a third re-seating within 24 hours, I ended up pouring the water into my water filter and going back to filtered water as I've used in my Nespresso machines for almost a decade.

title: PLEASE READ THIS REVIEW BEFORE YOU BUY OR INSTALL A COMBO UNIT LIKE THIS!!!
text:
THE MOST IMPORTANT THING I CAN SAY ABOUT THIS UNIT IS THAT HOW YOU INSTALL IT MAKES 100% DIFFERENCE BETWEEN IT BEING AN ABSOLUTE FAILURE AND IT BEING THE MOST USEFUL LAUNDRY APPLIANCE MONEY CAN BUY. Read the rest if you are considering a unit like this.

We have now had this washer/dryer for over a year so I feel like it is time for a review.

PROS:
- Put dirty laundry in at bedtime, take clean dry laundry out when you get up. Its that simple.
- It runs on 15amp household current so you can retrofit it into places
- It doesn't vent OUTSIDE so you can retrofit it into places
- It will keep tumbling your clothes to prevent wrinkles till you are ready for them.

CONS:
- The design on this unit is terrible. It was designed in a lab somewhere and never tested in the real world.
- IT IS NOT A VENTLESS DRYER. It has a vent, it just doesn't vent outside your hose like a normal dryer. There is a 2 inch round hole 2 inches below the cold water intake on the back. That is the vent. See my pictures attached to this review.
- The VENT on the back is located right next to the cold water house which causes the cold water hose to condense and drip water all over the place
- The hot humid air from the vent condenses on the wall and makes a giant mess.
- The top of the dryer gets HOT! The top right front corner gets above 130 degree F when in operation. It is hot enough to scald you from touching it. I measured it with a non-contact IR thermometer.
- It uses cold water to dry the clothes. If you live somewhere where water is an issue, this unit wastes a lot of clean water. Maybe find a way to collect and reuse it. It will contain lint but that's it. Based on my measurements, it uses about 8 gallons to dry your clothes.

Why would i give it 3 stars? Because I finally worked around these issues and once I did that, it works great any my wife loves it. We purchased this unit because it was supposed to be ventless and would run on a standard 15amp outlet. The plan was to install the unit directly inside our closet in the master bedroom. Having the washer dryer in the closet means that laundry goes nowhere but in the closet. There is no hauling it around in baskets or hampers. Wear it, wash it, wear it or hang it.

In the closet I removed the access panel for the hall bathroom tub and cut in a new washer hookup. I installed the LG unit and started learning the hard way that the ventless dryer vents a lot of steam out the back. I installed a sheet of thick plastic and funnel/host to catch the condensation and drain it to the floor pan drain. I installed a set of 80MM silent computer fans that are on a relay and run when the unit is running. All of that helped but didn't solve the problem. The dryer would run for hours and the clothes would not dry. I called LG to ask about this problem and they denied making a ventless dryer. They were totally useless.

Then I found a review of a similar unit online where the reviewer mentioned the unit was drying great and there was no steam coming from his vent but he also was using the floor drain instead of a wall drain. I immediately pulled my drain pipe from the wall and put it in a 5 gallon water jug on the floor. Magically most of the steam stopped and the clothes came out dry! I removed the unit, cut a new lower drain into my plumbing, and hooked it all back up. Like magic it all worked! There was less steam coming from the back but it still caused the cold water line to condense a lot of water. I solved this problem by wrapping the cold water hose in pipe insulation and getting it out of the way of the vent with a 90 degree adapter and some careful placement of the hoses.

=====================================
=====================================
So the bottom line is that this unit is only functional if you can drain the water into something that is no higher than half way up the unit. If your drain is any higher than half way up the back this unit is a total failure (minus 1 star). You should also keep in mind that it gets VERY hot to the touch and small children could get hurt by it (minus another 1 star).
=====================================
=====================================

title: Not a good solution for fridges with no ice maker.
text:
What I Like: It's a marvel how quickly it can produce ice. It's probably quicker than the specs say. It started spitting out cubes in probably 5 minutes. It's also pretty. You'd have no problem having this sit out. Perfect for a man cave or rec room where you want ice for cocktails or sodas. Would fit on top of a mini fridge. It would be a great gift for someone living in a dorm. Cubes are small enough to fit in any water bottle.

Here's what I don't like: Takes up a lot more counter space than the actual dimensions of the machine because it requires 6 inches of open ventilation space around it at all times. I posted a pic to show what that looks like with a standard size roll of paper towels for scale.

The ice cubes are watery. They don't feel like cubes made in a traditional freezer that are dry and stick to your skin. These feel like they've already started melting even when they are fresh. The unit is designed to catch the water as the cubes melt, and then remake that water into new cubes.

Part of the problem is there is virtually no insulation on the unit. I'm posting a pic of the lid, which shows it's just basic plastic. No rubber/magnetic seal like you'd see on a freezer to hold in cold air. Basically the same amount of insulation you'd have on an ice bucket at a hotel. Because of this, it isn't very efficient. It's basically constantly remaking ice from melting ice.

I got this because the freezer in my apartment doesn't have an ice maker, but I don't know how practical it would be for me to keep a machine running like that nonstop. If you're using it for that purpose, you'll probably want to dedicate an ice bucket in your freezer and transfer the cubes to it until it is full. Then unplug the ice maker until you need to make more. Kind of a pain in the neck. Also if you do that, the cubes are going to stick together because they are watery to start with. I posted a pic of how the ice cubes turned into one solid block of ice when I did that.

The description in the ad also describes applications for this machine which conflict with the warnings in the manual, which I have posted a picture of. It says specifically to not use it outside, in direct sunlight or with a high ambient temperature. That means using it outdoors at a BBQ is out. It also says you can't tip it and if you move it, you need to wait two hours for the coolant to settle before you use it. So I don't think it would work well for camping or even in an RV unless you were staying in the same place for awhile. Just not as portable as I envisioned it being.

It's basically a very expensive ice tray. You've got to really hate filling up ice trays for this purchase to make sense for that application.

Also, the internal basket doesn't hold that much ice and fills up quickly. So when they say in the ad it makes 25 lbs of ice in 24 hours, you'd have to be standing next to it for 24 hours constantly transferring ice to another container. It probably only holds 1 pound of ice at any given time.

For those reasons, this thing is going back.

title: Beware of floating debris in your water!
text: All instructions were followed successfully for installation and flushing. The filter has been installed in my Jenn-Air refrigerator for a couple of days.  Today my son told me there was something wrong with the water filter.  He had two glasses full of water and showed me what looked to be floating debris in the water.  He made the second glass to make sure the debris he viewed did not come from the glass.  It resembled plastic, or dead skin floating in the water.  I had just filled our 4L water boiler so I opened it up.  I used a flashlight to get a close look at the water.  It had what appeared to be hundreds of examples of this floating worm looking debris.  The debris is coming from the water filter.  I have never had a problem like this with any of our replacement filters in the past.  I would advise checking your "clear water" from this filter very closely.  I am processing a return now.

title: Looks good, but difficult to install to to design flaws.
text:
The unit came with a small dent on the front right side where you can see it. See photo. I would have given it a one star, but my wife didn’t notice it after the installation, so I gave it two stars. It wasn’t enough damage compared to the hassle of returning something that weighs over fifty pounds. The outside of the box didn’t have any visible damage, so I didn’t find the damage until I took everything apart.

Pros:
1. Looks good.
2. Good suction. I didn’t find the fan noise much different than other hoods.

Cons:
1. No installation instructions in the box. I found lousy installation instructions on their website. Their website has a place for a video, but there is no video.

2. One of the fans didn’t work on low. Then it worked on high, but made a noise. Found out it was rubbing the plastic cover. I had to use a longer sheet metal screw to fix it. It’s possible I warped it holding it up during installation, because I thought I tested everything before installation. Be careful not to touch the covers when trying to hold it up.

3. You have to drill holes for installation in preset locations (mentioned by others too), but the locations don’t give you enough tolerance. I had one screw almost on my front cabinet. Other manufacturers making units one sixth the price have a slot where you put up screws and slide the unit on them. I had to use nuts and bolts, so I have holes in the inside of my upper cabinet. One person had to hold the unit up while another put in the bolts. The manufacturer should have several pre-drilled spots with the wider holes to slide on to screws to easily solve this.

3. There’s a metal box near one of the screw locations making it difficult to work with with almost no room for your hand.

4. I have no idea why they have handles on the upper grease screens. It’s a waste of time to install them and they are not necessary. You should have a magnetic screwdriver to put on the handles.

5. The spacers above are painted and do not match the brushed stainless of the other parts. Unless you are short you would not see this or care.

title: Did not remove the chlorine taste
text:
I bought this because the filters made by Maytag were unavailable, and this had good reviews from other Amazon customers, and I thought American made would be good. I installed it (no leaks), flushed it out for about 5 minutes, and checked the water. It looked okay but tasted horrible - high chlorine just like our straight tap water. I flushed it a couple more times, and no improvement. I went ahead and used it thinking it might get better or I might get used to it. But after about a day and a half I still couldn't stand the taste, so put my old filter back in. The water taste immediately improved. I left the old filter in and ordered a different brand from Amazon. The documentation on this filter doesn't say anything about meeting any standards for contaminant removal. It only listed the operating temperature and pressures. Maybe they forgot to install the filtration media in mine and sent me a lemon, but I don't intend to try another one.

Update: Decided to cut open the filters and and look inside to see how they work (or don't). As shown in the photos, the Maytag filter on the left was built with the filtration cartridge as an insert that was sealed very well on the top and bottom to make sure the water goes through the filter material. Inside the Perfectpure filter (on the right in the photos), the top inlet was off center and poorly glued. This resulted in an incomplete seal to the filter media, so the incoming water just bypassed the filter and came right back out of the top with no filtration. The bottom of the Perfectpure filter was apparently sealed, but based on the small amount of glue it could have a similar manufacturing problem as the top seal. So the bottom line appears to be that if you are lucky and the glue and parts all line up when it is put together, then the Perfectpure filter will be fairly inexpensive and probably work okay as it has for some of the reviewers. But if quality control is bad as it was with mine, then you are just buying a very expensive piece of pipe.

title: A fix for the water leak.
text:
I’m responding to the review by Mumofmany as air vent was the leaking problem. Thank you Mumofmany!
I bought the same front load LG washer back in 2013. The leaking has been an intermittent problem since then.
I’ve had an LG service tech out to look who said not to fill the detergent higher than the normal fill line. We were sure to fill below that line and still had leaking issues that leaked on our 2nd story laundry room.
I bought the washer and dryer at Home Depot and they couldn’t help me either.
I literally saw water and foam coming out of the air vent in back of the washer toward the end of the wash cycle which is the rinse and spin cycle.

First, I removed the rubber gasket around the mushroom fitting in the air vent but that didn’t do the trick. I put the rubber gasket back.

I finally had enough of that and removed the vent cover on the back of the washer. I replaced air vent fitting with a 1-1/4” pvc connector. I needed to make the hole little bigger so I had a 1-1/4” knockout punch to do that.
I bought a 1-1/4” Electrical locknut to secure the connector. I connected the air vent hose to a threaded end of the 1-1/4” pvc plumbing connector to the washer. Then, I installed a reducer fitting to 3/4” and threaded a 3/4” hose connector to that and connected a 3/4” hose. I cut in a “Y” fitting to the 2” laundry drain pipe. At the “Y” fitting, I reduced the “Y” to 3/4” and threaded a 3/4” hose connector. Connected the hose and I haven’t had any more water leaking. All the vent water goes down my drain pipe now. I’m very satisfied with my solution!
I don’t think the original air vent was designed correctly. Thank you.

title: Whirlpool Part?
text:
This water valve was represented by the merchant as being an original Whirlpool part direct from the manufacturer. However, when I contacted Whirlpool to verify this was in fact an original part they could not validate the origin. In the absence of an FCA marking (factory certified part) or an FSP marking (factory specified part) it is questionable as to the source or approved application.

If you look at the attached photo you will notice that the seam connecting the cap and valve housing is not constructed in a workman like manner and unevenly joined together giving me reservations regarding the quality. Since this water device is under pressure and the failure could be costly in terms of water damage, I decided not to install it in my refrigerator.

I elected to purchase another product which was FSP certified from another merchant as I have more confidence in the quality of construction. Unfortunately, I could not return the initial water valve without paying shipping and a restock fee so I now have pretty nifty little door stop.

title: Very poor design.
text:
As a product designer for Eastman Kodak for over 35 years the design of this rail is absolutely terrible .

Simple radius along the corner where the extended tab meets the vertical section would've eliminated this problem altogether. designer of plastic parts know that sharp corners creates a fracture point where a smooth radius adds material and strengthens it. The product designer should be demoted to draftsperson .

The only way to solve this problem since a replacement part will not fix the problem is to actually mechanically secure the broken rail to the shelf .

Sure should be very self-explanatory .

The edges are smoothed out so I don't think anyone would be cut on the metal sticking up. I asked the seller for help on this and was told to use a pair of pliers to increase the bend. I bought a new waher hose that hooks up under my faucet. The washer works well enough. It also has a drying function which runs for 60 min. The clothes come out less wet but nowhere near dry, so you still have to hang your clothes or use a dryer.

I've also dicovered another issue with this unit. The rubber feet leave black marks on my floor everytime I slide it out. The manufacturer should have put wheels in the back considering this is a "portable washer". Because the seller provided wrong dimensions I can't intsall a sliding shelf or put it on wheeled cart. For the price this washer is fine, I'm just dissapointed that the seller is too proud to update their description to include correct washer dimensions - 2 stars because of that.

title: Horrible completely false advertising!
text:
I am heart broken and absolutely disgusted not only with this product but with LG as a whole. So I installed this upstairs by all the bedrooms, $2500 installation. So whether we could get our money back for this machine or not we are still out the 2500 bucks. Our humidity level rises 15% on a dry in cycle!!!!! Everything comes out smelling disgusting like it had been sitting in a washing machine sealed for like 2 days - just disgusting. And I am not talking big loads at all. Very small loads of laundry. We called customer service with our plumber here to try to fix the problem and I'm not kidding you the customer service said "oh yeah that's how this works it takes 5 to 6 hours for a wash and a dry cycle to go. Also you should be taking out half of your load before the dry cycle starts." What! I didn't buy a machine that washes and then switches to dry so I could come up when the wash cycle is over and take half of the load out so it could dry. Who is this guy? More importantly - why wasn't this listed in the product description and marketing material?

We also brought up comments about having the machine higher than the drain pipe so that the water can drain more efficiently during the dry cycle. He said that will not change it at all - just install it the way the manual says to have it installed and that is the best way to have it installed for the highest efficiency during the dry cycle.

DO NOT WAIST YOUR MONEY

**DEC 4 2018 EDIT**

We put the machine up on a pedestal so that the drain hose could drain directly down into our house washing machine drain. It worked great for a month (except the horrible smell of the dry clothes) and now we always get error 'OE'. Which means that our drain pump does not work. Called a couple companies that were helping us out with our issues and they said it's because the drain hose has to be going up out of the machine and then down along th floor and then up to the drain pipe. It doesn't make any sense. The whole hole hook up is designed to go down. why wouldn't they attach it going in the upward direction?

I add good smelling stuff to the wash every single time and it still came out dry smelling musky gross with a 3 hour dry time.

title: Burners don't work as well
text:
Ok, I had this installed into my stove with the replacement connector and burner. It works, but it felt like it was taking a lot longer to heat up. I swapped the element (burner) with the old one, and it works fine... Soo I looked at it, and the new one seems like its less "dense" area wise because the edges are rounded. I also took measurements and found that the newer coils had higher resistance. 21 ohm on the original vs 29 ohm on the replacement about a 30-40% difference there. I think that combined with the contact area being less is resulting in it not getting my pans up to temp.

Sending these back because the burners just don't get hot.

title: Catches loose lint, but is impractical to use.
text:
While remodeling a property that did not have a dryer vent in a utility room that is surrounded by solid concrete walls, I looked for a solution and bought this.
After using it once, I found myself avoiding doing laundry at this property. After using it a second time, I decided I have to look for a more sustainable solution and had a pipe installed through several solid concrete walls to vent hot humid air outside. While this product does pick up loose lint, it does nothing to the hot and extremely humid air that comes out of the dryer hose. That air gets recycled, but the room only gets hotter and more humid as the clothes are being dried and there's no place for the hot humid air to go. I found the extra humidity that sat in the utility room promoted mildew growth on clothes that were kept there on clothes hangers. If you live in a cool dry climate and need the extra heat and humidity, then this is a viable solution that has multiple upsides. I live in a tropical area and have to run the air conditioner constantly to cool down the house and lower the humidity. When the dryer is in use with this device, my air conditioner has to work extra hard to counteract the effects of the air exiting the clothes dryer. It is an impractical solution.

text:

I'm always careful to position it correctly and not to kink it, but the plastic is so thin I'm wondering how long it's SUPPOSED to last. Planned obsolescence???
Update May 30, 2016: I've downgraded my review to one star after the drain hose developed two more splits that are I can't reach to try to fix. I bought this machine in October 2015 and the hose ripped in March 2016. I managed to get a jury rigged fix that has held, but now there are more leaks/splits. This company has a higher priced model of this type of "dryer" which collects the water in a container at the front of the machine and I suspect it's because of the way the drain hose on this one splits. The only thing I use this machine for is to spin the water out of hand knitted clothes, and I don't use it every day, so this machine has probably been used a dozen times since I bought it. It spins really well and does just what I want, but the drain hose breaking is pathetic.
Update June 3, 2016: I contacted the seller (Laundry Alternative) and asked how to replace hose under "3 year comprehensive warranty" and the answer was "would you like a replacement?" I said, yes, and the replacement arrived today. That's great, but the hose is still flimsy. Buyer beware.

text: Product looked like the 16 year old original that was being replaced. Installed easily but I noticed i was getting a burning plastic smell inside the freezer when it went into defrost mode. I took everything back out of the freezer and verified nothing was touching the  defrost heater and it was installed correctly.  A few days latter the burning smell was getting stronger so I removed the defrost heater to inspect it.  Where the Glass tube covered Heating element enters the end connector the Housing is all burnt on both ends. The 16 year original does not show any signs of burning like this.  It looks to me like a quality issue with the material used to make the connection ends. This is possible fire danger as well  food & air contamination from this material burning and releasing fumes.

title: Good concept, poor execution; Faulty humidity sensor
text:
Waited for and bought this on Amazon lightning deal. I've installed it properly and have been using it for roughly 2 weeks. DIY Installation itself was tricky given the number of wires that need to be properly hooked up. I was able to do it just fine after watching couple of youtubes.

Understanding programming modes and functions was tricky too. You really have to read the instructions a few times to know what to do. Anyhow I was able to try out all three modes for few days each.

Now the bad part, The humidity sensor is way off and it's not a question of sensitivity level set. It triggers throughout the day and night automatically without anyone using the bathroom. It also fails to startup properly when someone is taking a shower. Another thing is you need a mode that allows for lights auto-on-off on PIR and fan auto-on-off on humidity level. There's isn't such a mode. Mode 2 comes close but I don't want fan to be switched on every time there's a motion.

The idea is so nice but it doesn't work. I'm going to return it and possibly try an alternative product.

title: Countertop Ice Maker
text: [[VIDEOID:3b4d863e1b1da1f55eaa99bf10f40ff5]] I use A LOT of ice. So much so that my refrigerator cannot keep up. So, I was excited to see available. When I read the ad, I remember seeing it say that it creates 26lbs of ice in 24 hours. Well, there is an exception to this. It does not store ice, so as it come out, you need to remove it in batches and put it in a freezer. So, if I wanted 26lbs of ice, I would have to stay up all night emptying into baggies to store it in my freezer. So, because it is not temp controlled, the ice does come out somewhat liquidie and then I store them in bags in the freezer and they sort of all mush together. title: Beware, my hinge was missing threads that I needed to re-install it with
text: I am adding this product review to hopefully save somebody else a lot of problems and time that I had. Bottom line is the hinge that I got was not threaded so I couldn’t use the original screws on the original hinge. I’ll upload some pictures that will hopefully show what I’m talking about but the bottom line was I saw hunted around for an hour or so trying to figure out a solution when I saw somebody else mentioned that they simply ran through longer screws and put a nut and washer on the backside. When I first read that I wasn’t sure I would have enough room behind the hinge and under the Cosmetic ring, but there was plenty of room. I love love these pictures and hopefully it wilMy hinge was missing threads that I needed to re-install it withl help somebody.  For the record, this huge worked on my whirlpool duet model number WFW9200S202. And there was really nothing wrong with the hinge per se so had I known it wasn’t threaded, I would’ve probably given it five stars… I just want folks to see this so I put the lower rating.

title: Particles come out!
text: Let me preface that I know you're suppose to let these soak to get the color out from the charcoal. I think the instructions said 15 minutes but I left them soaking for a day (I forgot about them). I dumped the water and rinsed as instructed. The dark color seemed to stop. However, little black particles kept coming out (see picture). I think the mesh part is too thin. I'd rather keep this out of my espresso machine so returned them. Something else I noted... my original Breville filter wasn't charcoal. Instead, it had some yellow stuff. I'll probably just buy the original for now.

title: Get what you pay for
text:
Installed easily in the GE model shown in photo. Beware the burst of water when you first activate your fridge to run water through the filter. Did not come with instructions, so I followed the instructions on the original GE filter I had installed. Ran 1.5 gallons of water through the system prior to use. I was able to purchase 3 of these for 1/2 the price of one GE filter. My system seemed to be oddly “over pressurized” with these filters and the water flow was not smooth and constant. When I removed this filter, it almost popped out of the socket, whereas the GE model is inserted and removed smoothly.

Upon further examination I found these were manufactured in China and only tested to NSF/ANSI 42. Original GE model is tested to 42 and 53. NSF 42 is for aesthetic impurities and flavor/odor. NSF 53 is for actual contaminants with a health effect. I returned this item and ordered the more expensive, albeit more effective GE manufacturer filter. I can’t put a price on my family’s health.

title: Does not perform as well as OEM filters
text:
We had been using the OEM LG water filters, but decided to try these to save some money. This was a mistake. We were surprised that, after we switched from the LG filter to the PurePlus filter, the water actually tasted WORSE. We immediately switched back to the OEM filter, which immediately recovered the taste quality of the water coming from the dispenser.

I then became curious to see if there was any difference in the performance data claimed by the manufacturer. The two photos show the Performance Data Sheet for each product. Both products reduced "Chlorine Taste and Odor". However, you can see that the LG OEM filter reduces many other contaminants, and based on our experience, that difference made a big difference in the taste of the water.

We will be sticking with LG OEM filters for the time being.

title: Washes clothes, but needs better engineering, broke after a month
text:
This product does a fine job of cleaning clothes; however, the engineering put into it was very lazy.

The bottom of the machine clearly has a spot where wheels COULD have been attached, or at least included as an optional addon, but were sadly not. Luckily it is light enough to pickup and move. The drain pipe could be longer or at least easily replaceable with a longer pipe, instead my options are limited as to where I can use this machine (in the bath tub exclusively)

The worst part is, after a month's use the spin dry has rubbed a hole through the side of the inner wall. I realize that you cannot put too much into the spinner otherwise it may be out of balance, but sometimes that does not even matter. I put a few clothing items in the spinner, level it off, and switch it on. The entire machine will STILL shake and I have to immediately switch it off to make adjustments. Sometimes this can go on 5-6 times before it finally will spin properly. These short instances are what rubbed a hole in the side wall. The sad part is that simply better engineering could have solved this. Less sway on the drum or a wider operation area would have done the trick.

I guess not much QA went into this product. Disappointed for the price.

title: The worst purchase I’ve ever made
text: My husband and I had to buy a new washer dryer unit in a hurry so when we came across this unit on sale at Best Buy we went for it. Since day one this unit has frustrated and disappointed me. As another reviewer mentioned, the washer doesn’t use nearly enough water. Sometimes the clothes on top (even with a half full load) are dry and dirty when I go to move them to the dryer and have not been washed at all! I have had laundry soap pods come out of the wash still intact. What’s the point of having a washing machine that doesn’t wash?!? I have a new baby and as such an increase in laundry needs which means I get to be irritated and frustrated by the washer twice as often! Now, the dryer. I’ve had many dryers in my life but none has distributed more lint into clothes than this bad boy. I am going to take a sweater to the dry cleaners today to see if it can be saved after having gone through the hell that is this drying cycle. The lint trap on this unit is on the door in such a way that lint practically explodes into your face when opening the door and no matter how often I clean the lint trap (literally more than once during a single drying cycle sometimes) I end up with clothes that are coated in lint. If I could go back in time to not purchase a single item in my whole life this washer dryer would be it. Don’t make the mistake I did!

So when installed if you go to wipe them down they slide around. I’m concerned this is eventually going to scratch the stove top that it’s covering. Would be nice if these locked together  and had some form of rubber bumper on the ends. title: Update- I have purchased 5 of these!!
text: I have been buying these from same seller off of amazon due to the "18-Month Warranty" So the first one I bought and installed for a customer I ended up getting a call a month later saying " Hey My dryer isn't heating" I said okay I'll come out and look at it again I go out and check it and find one of Thermal cut out's that came in the kit had went bad. I thought okay well maybe something just happened or I did something wrong when installing or something of that nature. I ordered another one from a different seller and installed dryer has been all good for several months. I purchased another couple and fixed a couple dryers and sold them 2 weeks after selling the one I get a call that the dryer is not heating so I go out and find the same thermal cut out fuse is blown on the heater again same as the customer I installed the kit in before, so I ordered one replaced it and dryer is all fine now. ( Costing me Money) a couple days later I get a call about another one of the dryers I sold no longer heating I go out and look at it same exact thing heater fine thermostat is fine but thermal cut out is blown not allowing ac voltage to flow through to give the heater 220 volts that it needs to heat. I got fed up contacted the seller trying to explain hey you know this is the 3rd time this has happened this is costing me money and time to keep fixing things like this instead of making money. Asked how the warranty process was going to work, he informed me to contact amazon and then amazon said to contact them that they do not handle anything to do with the warranty then he proceedes to tell me that I need to remove all the parts ( out of customers dryers) and send them back they will look at them and send a replacement kit after they receive it. ( You think someone that just paid money for a dryer wants to do this) ( Do you think someone buying and fixing appliances wants to continue costing them sevles money doing this) absolutly not, so I purchased another tco from a different buyer fixed the customers dryer and got ahold of amazon about the issues that I was facing I was sent a full replacement kit at no charge and i purchased another a day later. I have installed both kits the free one and the one I ordered into 2 different dryers at 2 different customer locations and have the EXACT SAME ISSUE and there is no warranty there is no taking care of it like it should be instead this time I was sent a refund!
